hook.text: In 2018, Snapchat redesigned its entire app. 1.2 million users signed a petition demanding it be changed back.

buildup.text: Snapchat's interface was already confusing for new users. The redesign aimed to separate friends from media content.

discovery.text: The new layout broke years of muscle memory. Users couldn't find their friends' stories or figure out the new navigation.

twist.text: Kylie Jenner tweeted 'does anyone else not open Snapchat anymore?' — Snap's stock dropped $1.3 billion in a single day.

climax.text: Snapchat partially reverted the redesign, but the damage was done. Millions had already migrated to Instagram Stories.

punchline.text: Redesigns that break user habits can be more dangerous than the problems they fix.

quiz.question: What went wrong with Snapchat's 2018 redesign?
options: It broke years of muscle memory and confused loyal users, driving them to competitors
The app crashed constantly after the update
Users wanted even more radical changes
